fault_hypothesis: The conversion of planned floating-point allocations to integers on line 72 uses simple truncation (`int()`), which discards fractional parts. This causes the sum of the final allocations to be less than the total available memory, violating the conservation principle.

constraints.intent: The sum of integer allocations must equal the total available memory.
The sum of the intermediate floating-point allocations should be equal to the total.
The total memory lost to truncation should be less than one full unit.
The sum of the final returned allocations must equal the total.
